patch.value: Post-install activation verification can load stale overwritten extension modules

Reinstalling an already loaded extension overwrites its files and immediately re-activates it in the same Node process. ESM module caching can retain the prior dependency module namespace, so a newly installed entrypoint importing a newly added named export reports a false activation failure even though the published and installed files are correct; a fresh pm process activates successfully. Reproduced with pm-changelog 2026.7.18: install reported MissingTagHistoryError absent, while tarball/installed release-context.js exported it and package doctor in a fresh process showed activation_status ok.

Reinstall/upgrade activation verification loads the just-installed module graph rather than stale ESM cache entries; an extension adding a named dependency export upgrades successfully in one process; unchanged extension install and fresh-process doctor behavior remain covered; diagnostics distinguish package defects from verifier cache state.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-18: all-status AND searches for post-install activation module cache and reinstall overwrite activation stale export found no canonical item. Related closed cache-reset lineage pm-8fxc covers invocation snapshot state, not ESM dependency cache during same-process overwrite; package lifecycle primitive lineage is pm-x6jf. Finding came from required latest pm-changelog generation and was proven not to be a pm-changelog package defect by published tarball hashes/exports plus fresh-process doctor activation.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Start broad SDK extension-authoring trust-boundary implementation

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation start 2026-07-23: selected after live 1,922-item all-status inventory, full canonical metadata/history review, and GitHub/release/security reconciliation. This item is actively edited in one coherent SDK/package authoring wave spanning scaffold contracts, flag arity, install validation, activation freshness, schema storage, renderer ownership, and public documentation; no duplicate item was created.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation evidence: install verification now supplies a caller-owned temporary snapshot root; discovery copies the complete extension directory and imports from a unique absolute graph, then the installer removes the snapshot after activation. A transitive entry-plus-dependency replacement regression test passed.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Verification evidence (2026-07-23): full instrumented repository suite passed 347 files / 6,146 tests with exact 100% statements (41,920/41,920), branches (29,630/29,630), functions (8,532/8,532), and lines (40,744/40,744). pnpm lint passed ESLint, zero jscpd clones, 100% source/export/member docstrings, package-boundary audit, and token-budget gate. pnpm security:scan found no credential-like tracked content; pnpm audit --prod found no known vulnerabilities. Packed artifact proof passed scripts/smoke-npx-from-pack.mjs (version 2026.7.23, 15 packages). A separate fresh /tmp consumer installed the npm tarball, initialized an isolated tracker, generated schema and renderer scaffolds, accepted repeated identical --capability values, rejected conflicting values, installed the scoped renderer, and returned extension doctor ok=true without a global renderer warning.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Consolidated release-gate evidence: all owned local gates passed, including exact coverage, version policy/sync, secret scan, packed npx smoke, and 88-command package-first dogfood. The aggregate command stopped only at greptile-review because this branch is intentionally not committed yet (reported: no committed code changes to review against main); review convergence will run after push and is not being misclassified as a code/test failure.

patch.value: Post-install activation now snapshots the complete extension directory into a fresh caller-owned module graph, preventing stale transitive ESM reuse; full coverage and reinstall proof pass.
